jokes.text: So I says, 'Super Collider? I just met her.'
jokes.setup: Robot comedian delivering a pun based on mishearing 'Super Collider'
jokes.punchline: The classic 'I just met her' response to scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic pun format but executed well for a robot character, economy is perfect

jokes.text: And then they built the Super Collider. Thank you. You've been great.
jokes.setup: Following up the pun with real-world consequence
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment that his joke somehow caused construction of actual Super Collider
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist follow-up that gives consequence to throwaway joke, very Futurama-esque

jokes.text: Our next standup's a veteran of four comedy traffic schools.
jokes.punchline: Comedy traffic schools as if they're legitimate comedy training
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay mixing traffic school with comedy credentials

jokes.text: Give it way up for Bobcat Zoidberg!
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'Bobcat Goldthwait' and 'Zoidberg'
jokes.explanation: Clever reference combining real comedian with character name

jokes.text: Earth. What a planet. On Earth, you enjoy eating a tasty clam. On my planet, clams enjoy eating a tasty you!
jokes.setup: Classic 'on my planet' setup with food comparison
jokes.punchline: Role reversal where clams eat people
jokes.explanation: Good structure and character-appropriate alien perspective joke

jokes.text: Maybe I'm not loud enough. On Earth, everybody is looking for a squid! On my planet...
jokes.setup: Zoidberg thinks volume is the problem, tries another planet joke
jokes.punchline: His desperation and misunderstanding of why the joke failed
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing Zoidberg's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Reload.
jokes.punchline: Treating bad comedy like ammunition that needs reloading
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and original metaphor for bombing comedian

jokes.text: So these three parasitic worms bore into a human's head. And they walk up to the bar and then... Wait! Stop! I've got more!
jokes.punchline: Desperately trying to continue despite being booed off
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing bombing comedian's desperation

jokes.text: My whole life I worked on that act. And they hated it.
jokes.punchline: Complete failure after lifelong dedication
jokes.explanation: Effective pathos mixed with comedy, character-appropriate disappointment

jokes.text: You're a crazy lobster doctor! You shouldn't be a comedian!
jokes.punchline: Bender's blunt assessment of career mismatch
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender bluntness, economical delivery

jokes.text: But comedy is in my valves.
jokes.punchline: Anatomically specific version of 'in my blood'
jokes.explanation: Nice character-specific twist on common expression

jokes.text: My Uncle Harold was a big star back in the era of silent holograms.
jokes.punchline: Futuristic twist on 'silent films' - silent holograms
jokes.explanation: Clever sci-fi twist on film history, good world-building humor

jokes.text: Why, I've been a fan since back when my hips were made of bone!
jokes.setup: Expressing how long he's been a fan
jokes.punchline: Referencing his ancient age via body part replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth line - absurd age reference with medical specificity

jokes.text: As it happens, I still have some of my original 78s.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning ancient record format
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency, reinforces his extreme age

jokes.text: This letter has to be very personal, so I'm writing it in my own ink.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg wanting to write personal letter
jokes.punchline: Using literal squid ink instead of pen ink
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor, perfectly Zoidberg biology joke

jokes.text: Norm and Sam and Sadie's boy? Remember?
jokes.setup: Trying to help uncle remember him
jokes.punchline: Generic Jewish family names applied to lobster alien
jokes.explanation: Good character humor mixing alien with Jewish cultural references

jokes.text: Now I'm the most important doctor at the company where I work.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to impress his uncle
jokes.punchline: Audience knows he's incompetent and not important at all
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor for fans who know Zoidberg's actual status

jokes.text: But my life is bereft of laughter.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal 'bereft' for simple concept
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Zoidberg's tendency toward overwrought language

jokes.text: What's that scribble-di-gook?
jokes.punchline: Dismissive made-up word for the elaborate letter
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with invented dismissive term

jokes.text: It's a letter from my rich nephew... who just might be my ticket out of this flophouse, he might.
jokes.setup: Harold's immediate change in attitude about the letter
jokes.punchline: Instantly seeing Zoidberg as meal ticket, redundant 'he might'
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - opportunistic relative

jokes.text: Yeah, you'd better run!
jokes.punchline: Tough guy posturing after the fact
jokes.explanation: Basic tough guy posturing humor, establishes character as washed up

jokes.text: P.S: cashier's check.
jokes.punchline: Specific financial demand in postscript
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Harold's opportunism and specificity

jokes.text: I'm kidding. That never happens.
jokes.setup: Warning about no refunds if tourists get discovered
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation - it never actually happens
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with economic delivery

jokes.text: Fox uses searchlights to blind pilots... then film the resulting plane crashes.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dark and unethical content creation method
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about media industry ethics

jokes.text: Ahead... you'll see the home of Mel Gibson, star of the hit film Bravehead.
jokes.punchline: Future version of 'Braveheart' with head pun
jokes.explanation: Simple pun on famous movie title, adequate but not inspired

jokes.text: Are you my hot-water heater?
jokes.punchline: Robot soap opera star confusing robot types
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific confusion humor, economical delivery

jokes.text: Unless you can heat water to 212 degrees, I'm not interested.
jokes.punchline: Very specific temperature requirement for robot friendship
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic humor with specific technical requirements

jokes.text: That was the other guy. My name's Boiler.
jokes.punchline: Pretending to be a water-heating robot
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's opportunistic lying

jokes.text: Nice work, Boiler. Thanks. And call me Bender.
jokes.setup: Calculon accepting the lie, Bender's response
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing his real name after the deception worked
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Boiler bit, shows Bender's casual attitude to deception

jokes.text: Next time you see me, don't be surprised if I've eaten.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg going to meet his uncle
jokes.punchline: Low expectations - just hopes to have eaten by next meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg poverty humor with understated delivery

jokes.text: Uncle Zoid! You look young enough to be thrown back.
jokes.punchline: Fishing reference applied to lobster relative
jokes.explanation: Excellent species-specific humor, perfect character integration

jokes.text: Rich nephew! Come over here and give your uncle a nice, big meal.
jokes.punchline: Expecting meal instead of hug, showing his opportunism
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Harold's priorities

jokes.text: So, here we are. A still-famous film comedian... And a rich, respected doctor with surviving patients. Eating in a restaurant, as we both often do.
jokes.punchline: Multiple lies about both their statuses, awkward attempt at normalcy
jokes.explanation: Good layered irony showing both characters' delusions and desperation

jokes.text: That dream dies now! You're unfunny and untalented!
jokes.punchline: Harold's brutal, immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with economical brutal honesty

jokes.text: That's why you're perfect for drama!
jokes.punchline: Somehow this makes him perfect for drama
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and backhanded compliment structure

jokes.text: I'll be back on top after 50 miserable years... of fame.
jokes.punchline: Describing 50 years of fame as 'miserable'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Harold's jaded perspective on success

jokes.text: This film has a juicy part for you... if you completely finance it with your money.
jokes.punchline: The 'juicy part' is being the financier
jokes.explanation: Good scam artist humor with clear misdirection

jokes.text: What can I get you? Is bread free? Yeah. We'll split an order.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking for order after big Hollywood deal
jokes.punchline: Harold's extreme cheapness - splitting free bread
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing extreme cheapness after claiming success

jokes.text: I've been here a day, and already I'm a Hollywood phony.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg reflecting on his Hollywood experience
jokes.punchline: Rapid transformation into stereotypical Hollywood fake
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Hollywood corruption

jokes.text: Since when have you been in the biz? Long enough, little man. Long enough.
jokes.punchline: Bender's affected Hollywood veteran response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender adopting Hollywood attitude

jokes.text: Get this. For a scant $1 million investment... you can be the star.
jokes.punchline: Describing million dollars as 'scant'
jokes.explanation: Good con man language minimizing huge investment

jokes.text: No. No, I don't like the font.
jokes.punchline: Rejecting based on typography instead of content
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd actor behavior, very Calculon-like superficiality

jokes.text: I can guarantee anything.
jokes.punchline: Harold's casual admission of complete dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and reveals character's shameless dishonesty

jokes.text: That makes sense. Wink, wink.
jokes.punchline: Saying 'wink, wink' instead of actually winking
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist literalization of common expression

jokes.text: No, I didn't. Raise middle finger.
jokes.punchline: Literally directing actor to flip someone off
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the literalization theme

jokes.text: I said this is a talkie! You've got to emote more! And you extras? Wave your arms! What is this, a morgue?
jokes.setup: Harold directing actors in dramatic scene
jokes.punchline: Demanding over-the-top silent movie acting in sound film
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Harold's outdated directing style

jokes.text: Just because it's a dramatic scene... doesn't mean you can't do a little comedy. Throw a pie, for God's sake!
jokes.punchline: Suggesting slapstick in dramatic scene
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Harold's comedy background

jokes.text: Editing is a long and expensive process. We spent all the money on pies, so it'll be ready Friday.
jokes.punchline: Budget blown on pie gags, callback to pie direction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to pie joke with absurd budget priorities

jokes.text: Pay the valet the 2 bucks. No! It's the principle of the thing. I see a parking lot ahead.
jokes.punchline: Fry's extreme cheapness causing them to miss the event
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Fry's misguided priorities

jokes.text: He's a visionary! Everyone walked out. They hated it.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradictory evidence of total failure
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between delusion and reality

jokes.text: Plagues have had better opening nights.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to biblical disasters having better reception
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and originality in describing failure

jokes.text: That's why I used the qualifier 'practically.'
jokes.punchline: Harold hiding behind semantic technicality
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier promise - lawyer-like weaseling out of commitment

jokes.text: That's why I used the qualifier 'practically.'
jokes.punchline: Harold's lawyerly defense of his weasel words
jokes.explanation: Good scam artist defense showing attention to weasel words

jokes.text: Oy! Now he emotes.
jokes.punchline: Harold noting good acting only appears during real anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to directing notes with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: That's the Emmy of movie awards!
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Golden Globe as television-level award
jokes.explanation: Great original insult with perfect character specificity

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Sylvester Stallone!
jokes.punchline: Recognizing celebrity also stuck in tar
jokes.explanation: Good absurd celebrity cameo in dire situation

jokes.text: No, I'm not. I'm an even bigger liar than you!
jokes.setup: Assumption that Stallone is movie star
jokes.punchline: Admission of being complete fraud
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hollywood phoniness theme

jokes.text: My career went down the day they invented Smell-A-Vision.
jokes.punchline: Futuristic technology reveal ruining action star
jokes.explanation: Excellent original sci-fi gag with character specificity

jokes.text: As a pathetic has-been and forgotten relic, they remember me!
jokes.punchline: Harold's bitter acknowledgment of how he's remembered
jokes.explanation: Good bitter character moment with self-awareness

jokes.text: No! This is one death I won't be responsible for. Zoidberg away!
jokes.setup: Harold ready to give up and die
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's heroic moment with catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment with memorable catchphrase

jokes.text: I've had so many face-lifts, they finally lifted it right off my body! It's true! It's true!
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of excessive plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of real celebrity characteristic to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Here comes Jack Nicholson's DNA, reconstituted in a gorilla body!
jokes.punchline: Futuristic resurrection in wrong body
jokes.explanation: Excellent sci-fi absurdity with celebrity specificity

jokes.text: My only marketable skill is to occupy space.
jokes.setup: Explaining his job as seat filler
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of his worth
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with perfect economy

jokes.text: Now I know how a Pez dispenser feels.
jokes.setup: Billy Crystal's head hosting awards show
jokes.punchline: Comparing decapitated state to candy dispenser
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about disembodied head situation

jokes.text: It's some kind of hollow tube devoid of human life. The L.A. subway! We can blast our way in and escape!
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's empty LA subway system
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure with real-world observation

jokes.text: What was that? A tar dolphin? Or a tar shark?
jokes.setup: Something moving in the tar pit
jokes.punchline: Fry's attempts to categorize impossible tar creatures
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry confusion about impossible situations - tries to apply logic

jokes.text: Star Trek: The Pepsi Generation. They Call Me Mr. Pibb. And Snow White and the 7 Ups.
jokes.punchline: Movies entirely named after products
jokes.explanation: Good product placement satire with clever movie title parodies

jokes.text: Play along, like with Marisa Tomei.
jokes.punchline: Reference to real Oscar conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference to real Hollywood controversy

jokes.text: But tonight I realize what's really important is to win two Oscars!
jokes.setup: Touching speech about what's really important
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by doubling down on superficiality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character consistency

jokes.text: Why are you upset? It's less fraudulent for you to have it.
jokes.punchline: Harold's logical justification for the scam
jokes.explanation: Good character logic and ironic reasoning

jokes.text: I respect and admire Harold Zoid too much to beat him to death.
jokes.punchline: Respectful reason for not committing murder
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Calculon's dramatic sensibilities

jokes.text: Oh, pardon me. I see you're with Mr. Stallone.
jokes.setup: Bouncer initially refusing entry to the party
jokes.punchline: Suddenly respectful when seeing Stallone from the tar pit
jokes.explanation: Pays off the earlier Stallone tar pit gag with Hollywood status worship
